title: ～と言わんばかりに
titleEn: Indicates an action or behavior that strongly implies a certain unspoken message or feeling.
structure: Verb-volitional + と言わんばかりに, い-Adjective + と言わんばかりに, な-Adjective + だと言わんばかりに, Noun + だと言わんばかりに
structureEn: Verb-volitional + と言わんばかりに, い-Adjective + と言わんばかりに, な-Adjective + だと言わんばかりに, Noun + だと言わんばかりに
explanation: Indicates an action or behavior that strongly implies a certain unspoken message or feeling. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～と言わんばかりに grammar point is used to express that someone's action or behavior is so strong that it's as if they are saying something without actually saying it. It can be translated as 'as if to say' or 'almost as though stating'. This form is often used when the speaker wants to convey speculation about someone's unvoiced thoughts or feelings based on their behavior.

title: ～と言わんばかりの Noun
titleEn: Expresses the concept of 'as if to say'; 'almost says'; 'suggests'.
structure: Verb-plain form + と言わんばかりの Noun
structureEn: Verb-plain form + と言わんばかりの Noun
explanation: Expresses the concept of 'as if to say'; 'almost says'; 'suggests'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～と言わんばかりの Noun grammar point is used to express that someone's action or a situation suggests a certain message, even though it is not explicitly said. It can be translated as 'as if to say', 'almost says', or 'suggests'. It is typically used to convey an unspoken message through someone's demeanor or behavior.

title: ～ながらに
titleEn: Expresses a state or condition continuing just as it is (e.g. 'from birth', 'while crying', 'in the old way').
structure: Common set phrases: 生まれながらに, 涙ながらに, 昔ながらに, 子供ながらに, etc.
structureEn: Common set phrases: 生まれながらに, 涙ながらに, 昔ながらに, 子供ながらに, etc.
explanation: Expresses a state or condition continuing just as it is (e.g. 'from birth', 'while crying', 'in the old way'). (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～ながらに grammar point is used to indicate a condition that remains unchanged from some starting point or in a certain manner. It appears in set phrases such as 生まれながらに (from birth), 涙ながらに (while crying), 昔ながらに (in the old/original way), 子供ながらに (even though [still] a child), etc.

title: ～ながらの Noun
titleEn: Indicates one action or state happening at the same time as the noun in question (e.g. 'dinner while watching TV').
structure: Verb-ますstem (or plain minus る) + ながら + の + Noun
structureEn: Verb-ますstem (or plain minus る) + ながら + の + Noun
explanation: Indicates one action or state happening at the same time as the noun in question (e.g. 'dinner while watching TV'). (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～ながらの Noun grammar point is used to modify a noun by describing an action happening simultaneously. For example, テレビを見ながらの夕食 means 'dinner while watching TV.' It can be translated as 'while ~ing' or 'during ~.'

title: ～ながらも
structure: Verb stem + ながらも, い-Adjective + ながらも, な-Adjective/Noun + ながらも
structureEn: Verb stem + ながらも, い-Adjective + ながらも, な-Adjective/Noun + ながらも
explanation: Means 'even though' or 'despite'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～ながらも grammar point is used to express contrast, highlighting the difference between what is usually expected from a situation and what actually happens. It can be translated as 'even though' or 'despite.'

title: ～なくはない
titleEn: Expresses the idea 'it's not that it's impossible' or 'it's not that I can't'.
structure: Verb-ないform + なくはない, い-Adjective－い + くはない, な-Adjective + ではなくはない, Noun + ではなくはない
structureEn: Verb-ないform + なくはない, い-Adjective－い + くはない, な-Adjective + ではなくはない, Noun + ではなくはない
explanation: Expresses the idea 'it's not that it's impossible' or 'it's not that I can't'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～なくはない grammar point implies that while something may not be your first choice or not guaranteed, it's not outright impossible. Translates to 'It's not that it's impossible,' 'It's not that I don't/can't ~.'

title: ～なくもない
structure: Verb-ないform + なくもない, い-Adjective－い + くもない, な-Adjective + でもない, Noun + でもない
structureEn: Verb-ないform + なくもない, い-Adjective－い + くもない, な-Adjective + でもない, Noun + でもない
explanation: Expresses 'not impossible' or 'might as well'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～なくもない grammar point is used to suggest that something is not necessarily preferred or likely, but it's also not out of the question. It can often translate to 'It’s not a bad idea' or 'It might be feasible.'

title: ～なら～なりに
titleEn: Means 'if it is X, then it's X in its own way', emphasizing unique qualities.
structure: Noun/Adjective + なら + (same Noun/Adjective) + なりに
structureEn: Noun/Adjective + なら + (same Noun/Adjective) + なりに
explanation: Means 'if it is X, then it's X in its own way', emphasizing unique qualities. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～なら～なりに grammar point shows that something has its own particular way or style of being, based on that condition. It often conveys a positive acceptance or appreciation of the situation 'in its own way.'

title: ～には及ばない
titleEn: Means 'no need to go that far' or 'not necessary'.
structure: Verb-dictionary form + には及ばない
structureEn: Verb-dictionary form + には及ばない
explanation: Means 'no need to go that far' or 'not necessary'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～には及ばない grammar point is used to show that an action or degree of effort is unnecessary or excessive. It often translates to 'there’s no need to...' or 'it doesn’t warrant...' in English.

title: ～に堪えない
titleEn: Indicates something is unbearable or not worth doing (e.g., 'unbearable to watch', 'unbearable to listen to').
structure: Verb-dictionary form + に堪えない, Noun + に堪えない
structureEn: Verb-dictionary form + に堪えない, Noun + に堪えない
explanation: Indicates something is unbearable or not worth doing (e.g., 'unbearable to watch', 'unbearable to listen to'). (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～に堪えない grammar pattern is used when something is so unpleasant, awful, or painful that one cannot tolerate it. Common verbs include 見るに堪えない, 聞くに堪えない, 読むに堪えない, etc., meaning 'unbearable to see/hear/read.'
